headline: Brain Injury Erased His Career, But Not His Gift
meta_description: A Seattle man's brain injury took his 20-year career, but left his talent for fixing bicycles intact—now he repairs and donates bikes for free.
summary_short: After a brain injury stripped away memories of two decades of work, a Seattle man discovered an unexpected path forward through the skill his hands still remembered.
body:
A Seattle man faced an unimaginable loss when a brain injury erased the career he'd spent 20 years building. The professional expertise, the relationships, the identity tied to that work—all gone from his memory. Yet something remarkable remained intact: his hands knew how to fix bicycles.

Instead of dwelling on what was lost, he channeled this preserved skill into something meaningful. He began repairing broken bikes and giving them away for free to people in his community. Each repaired bicycle represents a small resurrection—machines that might have ended up discarded now serve others again.

The work offers more than practical transportation. For him, it's become a form of healing and purpose. For recipients, it's unexpected generosity. His story reveals how resilience sometimes arrives not through fighting our circumstances, but through discovering what remains when everything else falls away.
